reachability_artifacts: cmd/sworn/doctor_test.go:TestDoctorSchemaManifestRendersGradedAndAdvisory — drives cmdDoctor (the CLI-invoked entry point sworn doctor calls), asserts Group 1b renders all 9 schemas with correct GRADED/ADVISORY status

cmd/sworn/doctor_test.go:TestDoctorSchemaSkewFiresOnFixture — drives cmdDoctor with an injected skew fixture (baton.SetSchemaMapForTest), asserts baton/schema-skew and the unclassified entry both render [WARN], never [OK]

Manual smoke: `go run ./cmd/sworn doctor` live against this repo — Group 1b output captured in journal.md; git-stash A/B diff of the doctor CLI's [ERROR] line set confirms zero regression to existing groups (75 pre-existing, unrelated ERRORs, byte-identical before/after)

effort_complexity.rationale: Declarative manifest + one doctor section + skew warn; low breadth, low depth. Current-valid vocab (chore); S12 migrates to quick.

design_decisions.choice: Vendored schema names + $id + version are 100% derived from schemas.SchemaMap (go:embed ground truth) — zero hand-typed name list. Only the graded/advisory classification is a small (9-entry) hand-authored table, cross-checked against SchemaMap by SchemaSkew() so it cannot silently drift.
design_decisions.rationale: Narrow, local, easily revisited. Satisfies R-01's 'source from vendored files where possible' — the one thing that can't be mechanically derived (which schemas are graded vs advisory) is compensated by a fail-loud skew check rather than a second fragile parser.

design_decisions.choice: Skew is surfaced as [WARN], not [ERROR] — it does not flip cmdDoctor's hasError / exit code. A structural failure (malformed embedded schema JSON) still returns levelError and gates, fail-closed.
design_decisions.rationale: Matches AC-02's literal wording ('clearly-flagged WARN/non-OK') and the existing convention that Groups 2/3/4 are visibility-only while Group 1's embedded-prompt/pin checks are the ones that gate exit code — doctor's own WARN/ERROR convention, already established.

design_decisions.choice: Rejected parsing internal/adopt/baton/VERSION's free-text 'schemas-added: ... vendored-advisory' line as the machine source for the advisory set; used a small explicit Go table + SchemaSkew() against SchemaMap instead.
design_decisions.rationale: Implementation-detail, no external contract. Free-text VERSION scraping is exactly the fragile prose-scraping pattern ADR-0011 already deleted elsewhere (the verifier-verdict prose scraper); the explicit table + skew check satisfies R-01's 'where possible' without adding a second, more fragile parser.
